She told me the contract had been signed.
They asked if we could meet halfway.
Andrea, thanks for meeting. I spoke to my director yesterday, and he said we could offer you fifteen percent if the volume is high enough.
That is a good start, Anders. But last month you told me the rate would be fixed for two years. Is that still in the contract?
It is. The clause is already in the draft. My director asked if you could commit to fifty room nights a month.
We can meet halfway. If we commit to forty, would you sign off on the fifteen percent and renew for two years?
Let me be honest -- I need something in return. The deadline for the new season is tight.
Understood. I will confirm forty room nights in writing today, so you have leverage with your director. That gives us both a binding agreement we can trust.
That works. I will update the draft and send it over. Once we both sign off, the partnership is official.
Perfect. I appreciate how clearly you have laid out the terms, Anders. This is exactly how a good partnership should start.
Let us meet halfway on this.
He said it would be fine.
Once we sign off, it is done.
I will send you the draft today.
You said the rate would be fixed for two years.
My director told me the deadline was Friday.
Can we meet halfway on the volume?
Once we both sign off, the deal is binding.
I will confirm all the terms in writing today.

Hi Andrea, it is Anders. Quick update after my meeting. I spoke to head office this morning, and they said the fifteen percent was approved, but only if we sign before the end of the month. My director also told me that the two-year term would not be a problem, as long as the volume stays above forty room nights. Oh, and legal asked whether you could review clause seven, about cancellations. They said it needed one small change. Could you look at the draft and let me know if the terms work for you? If they do, I will send the final version to sign. Thanks a lot, and talk soon.

Welcome to this short segment on reporting a negotiation. When you agree something important, never rely on memory. Report it back, and write it down. In English, we use reported speech. Notice how the tense moves back one step. If your partner says, the rate will be fixed, you report, he said the rate would be fixed. If they say, we have approved the discount, you report, they said they had approved the discount. For questions, drop the inversion and use if or whether: they asked if we could meet halfway. Two small tips: use tell with a person, told me, told us, but use say without one, he said that. And always confirm the terms out loud before you sign. A deal that is reported clearly is a deal that gets renewed.
